Politics.headline: Israel renews Lebanon strikes and forces Syria border crossing closed
Politics.detail: Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed at least 15 people on Sunday, a day after Israel threatened to hit Lebanon's main border crossing with Syria, forcing it to close. Israeli military chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir visited troops in southern Lebanon on Sunday and pledged to intensify strikes against Hezbollah.

Politics.headline: Ukraine, Syria agree to cooperate on security, Zelenskyy says
Politics.detail: Ukraine and Syria pledged greater security cooperation in talks on Sunday, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said as Kyiv seeks to promote its military expertise across the region following the outbreak of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. Zelenskyy met with his Syrian counterpart, Ahmed al-Sharaa, in Damascus.

Politics.headline: Trump seeks historic $1.5 trillion for military in Congress budget request
Politics.detail: President Trump is requesting $1.5 trillion for the military, which is the largest request in decades and accompanies cuts to domestic programs.

Politics.headline: Trump warns Iran to reopen strait of Hormuz by Tuesday or face 'hell'
Politics.detail: President Trump has warned Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday or face severe consequences. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital shipping lane for oil and other goods.

Politics.headline: New UK farm inheritance tax rule will cause 'significant challenges', say accountants
Politics.detail: Accountants are warning that a new UK farm inheritance tax rule will cause significant challenges. The new rule could impact how farms are passed down to the next generation.

Finance & Economy.headline: Jim Rickards: The AI Crash Won't Stay in Silicon Valley - It Will Hit the Entire Economy, and Most Americans Don't Know They're Exposed
Finance & Economy.detail: Economist Jim Rickards argues that America's GDP growth has become dangerously dependent on AI investment and that when the bubble bursts, the damage will reach every corner of the economy, triggering a broad recession affecting construction workers, energy employees, manufacturers, and communities across the country.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global bond markets rattled as Middle East conflict threatens financial stability
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing American-Israeli and Iranian conflict continues to exert profound pressure on the global financial system, impacting government debt and international bond sales, with central banks worldwide selling approximately $82 billion in U.S. Treasuries since the start of the conflict.

Finance & Economy.headline: Private credit growth drops to record low, near-term recovery unlikely
Finance & Economy.detail: Bangladesh's private sector credit growth fell to a historic low of 6.03% in February, driven by prolonged political instability and a high interest rate regime, with bankers and business leaders suggesting that due to the Iran war, a recovery in credit growth is unlikely in the near future.

Finance & Economy.headline: Report: Iran war driving up funeral costs in the UK
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing conflict involving Iran is contributing to rising funeral costs in the UK.

Finance & Economy.headline: US average fuel price passes $4 a gallon for first time in four years amid Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: The average fuel price in the US has surpassed $4 a gallon for the first time in four years, driven by the ongoing conflict involving Iran.

Technology.headline: Pentagon Finds Success with Patent Holiday Program
Technology.detail: The Defense Department launched a program in late January offering companies no-fee patent licenses to accelerate technology development in areas like AI, biomanufacturing, and hypersonics, and it has already generated a lot of interest. The program aims to increase the visibility and utilization of the department's vast patent portfolio, which has not been generating significant revenue despite substantial research investments.

Technology.headline: Helium shortages cloud tech prospects
Technology.detail: Helium shortages and higher electricity tariffs driven by the US-Iran war, along with the US tariffs, are still weighing on the technology sector. BIMB Securities maintains a cautious outlook for the second half of financial year 2026 (2H26), as geopolitical tensions and the associated inflationary pressures on industrial gases and energy could eventually dampen the current capital expenditure (capex) momentum.

Technology.headline: Showcase your groundbreaking tech at the Indonesia Technology Excellence Awards 2026
Technology.detail: The nomination period runs from 6 April to 26 June 2026. Winners will be celebrated at a prestigious ceremony in Jakarta in October, offering a platform to showcase your breakthrough technologies and join Indonesia's leading tech trailblazers.

Technology.headline: Russia's VPN crackdown caused bank outage, Telegram founder says
Technology.detail: Telegram's Durov says Russia triggered payment system problem by blocking VPNs. Russia's VPN crackdown caused bank outage, Telegram founder says.

Technology.headline: Artemis II Mission: Orion Has Left Earth's Orbit on a Trajectory to the Moon
Technology.detail: The Orion spacecraft, part of the Artemis II mission, has successfully left Earth's orbit and is now on its way to the Moon. This marks a significant step in the mission to send humans back to the lunar surface.

Science.headline: AI Breakthrough Cuts Energy Use by 100x While Boosting Accuracy
Science.detail: Researchers have developed a new approach that significantly reduces energy consumption in AI, potentially slashing it by up to 100 times while maintaining or improving accuracy.

Science.headline: Scientists Find Hidden Brain Cells Helping Deadly Cancer Grow
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a surprising weakness in glioblastoma, one of the deadliest brain cancers, by identifying hidden brain cells that aid its growth.

Science.headline: New pill shows promise in lowering high blood pressure
Science.detail: A new pill called baxdrostat is showing strong results in lowering dangerously high blood pressure in people who don’t respond to standard treatments; in a large global trial, patients saw their blood pressure drop by nearly 10 mmHg.

Science.headline: A Piece of Moss Helped Investigators Prosecute a Grave-Robbing Ring
Science.detail: A piece of moss was crucial evidence in prosecuting a grave-robbing ring.

Science.headline: NASA astronauts return to Earth after 9 months in space
Science.detail: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams have returned to Earth after being stuck in space for more than nine months; their SpaceX capsule parachuted into the Gulf of Mexico early Tuesday evening.

Health.headline: Raw dairy farm recalls some cheese products as FDA investigates E. coli outbreak
Health.detail: A California producer of raw milk and cheese products is recalling some of its products under pressure from federal officials due to an E. coli outbreak.

Health.headline: FDA grants speedy approval to Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill for obesity
Health.detail: The FDA has granted speedy approval to Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill for obesity, marking a significant development in treatment options.

Health.headline: Deepfake X-rays created by AI are convincing enough to fool doctors and AI models
Health.detail: AI-generated deepfake X-rays are now so realistic that they can deceive both doctors and AI models, posing potential challenges for medical diagnostics. In tests, radiologists had limited success identifying fake images, especially when they didn't know they were looking at AI-generated images.

Health.headline: HHS To Examine Health Effects Of Tiny Plastic Particles That Leach Into Water
Health.detail: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) will investigate the potential health impacts of microscopic plastic particles that contaminate water sources.

Health.headline: E. coli outbreak linked to raw cheddar cheese grows as two more illnesses reported
Health.detail: An E. coli outbreak linked to raw cheddar cheese has expanded, with two additional cases reported, raising concerns about food safety.

Sports.headline: Mullins family shifts longtime allegiance from Michigan to UConn for national championship game
Sports.detail: The Mullins family, previously Michigan fans, are now supporting UConn in the national championship game.

Sports.headline: Dodgers Star Mookie Betts Lands on IL After Suffering Oblique Injury
Sports.detail: Mookie Betts, a star player for the Dodgers, has been placed on the injured list due to an oblique injury.

Sports.headline: UCLA Completes Near-Perfect Season, Beats South Carolina to Win 1st Women's NCAA Hoops Title
Sports.detail: UCLA defeated South Carolina to win their first Women's NCAA Hoops title, capping off a near-perfect season.

Sports.headline: PSV is Dutch champion for 3rd straight year after Feyenoord fails to win
Sports.detail: PSV secured their third consecutive Dutch championship after Feyenoord was unable to win their match.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026 RCB vs CSK: Lots of people have forgotten that we are champions: RCB vice-captain Jitesh Sharma
Sports.detail: RCB vice-captain Jitesh Sharma reminds everyone that RCB are champions in the context of the IPL 2026 match against CSK.

Entertainment.headline: Movie stars join forces to buy Auckland film and TV studio
Entertainment.detail: Jason Momoa, Taika Waititi and Jamie Lee Curtis have joined forces to purchase an Auckland film and TV studio, making headlines in the entertainment industry. The move signifies a major investment in the screen industry.

Entertainment.headline: William Shatner slams AI fake news claiming he's dying, had heated Erika Kirk confrontation.
Entertainment.detail: William Shatner has criticized AI-generated fake news that falsely claimed he was dying and also addressed a fabricated heated confrontation with Erika Kirk. Shatner is actively refuting these false reports.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby has been found liable in a sexual assault case and is ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972. This ruling marks a significant development in the long-standing legal battles against Cosby.

Entertainment.headline: Zendaya, Robert Pattinson step out at 'The Drama' premiere
Entertainment.detail: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson appeared together at 'The Drama' premiere. Both actors have been garnering attention for their roles and public appearances.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Declares Zendaya 'Looks Amazing' as They Step Out in Paris Amid Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland praised Zendaya's appearance as they stepped out in Paris for 'The Drama' premiere, amidst ongoing speculation about their marriage. Holland's public admiration for Zendaya continues to fuel interest in their relationship.

Crime & Justice.headline: Christchurch bishop's plea over stolen brass eagle: 'Happy to forgive'
Crime & Justice.detail: A brass eagle lectern gifted to a small Canterbury church more than a century ago has been stolen, prompting an Easter plea from the Anglican bishop for its return; the bishop stated they are happy to forgive the thief but earnestly plead for the return of their treasured item.

Crime & Justice.headline: Meloni hails arrest of top crime family suspect after raid at an Italian resort
Crime & Justice.detail: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has praised police after the arrest of alleged organized crime boss Roberto Mazzarella, who is one of Italy's most wanted fugitives.

Crime & Justice.headline: Tiger Woods released on bail hours after arrest on suspicion of DUI
Crime & Justice.detail: US golfer Tiger Woods was arrested in relation to a rollover crash in Florida that did not cause any significant injuries and was later released on bail.

Crime & Justice.headline: Woman charged in root beer float murder orders hit on best friend
Crime & Justice.detail: Marsha Allen, already charged in the death of her husband, is accused of hiring someone to burglarize her home and implicate her daughter in the crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former West Virginia Correctional Officer Pleads Guilty to Conspiracy Against Inmates' Rights
Crime & Justice.detail: A former West Virginia correctional officer has admitted guilt in a conspiracy against inmates' rights.

Education.headline: CBSE plan for 2026-27: Three-language formula from Class 6; two levels of maths, science for Class 9
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is planning a significant structural shift in mathematics and science, introducing a two-level system starting in the 2026-27 academic session, and a three-language formula from Class 6.

Education.headline: Public access to CT college course syllabi threatened under new proposal
Education.detail: A new proposal threatens public access to college course syllabi in Connecticut.

Education.headline: University of Wyoming selects Reeves as next president
Education.detail: The University of Wyoming has selected Reeves as its next president.

Education.headline: AI Is Supercharging Cyberattacks. What Schools Need to Know and Do
Education.detail: Many school districts are ill-prepared to defend themselves against AI-powered cyberattacks, and some experts are unsure what role the tech should play in cybersecurity.

Education.headline: Georgia teachers union boss blames school choice for public schools being 'grossly underfunded'
Education.detail: The head of the Georgia teachers union claims that school choice initiatives are responsible for the significant underfunding of public schools.

Lifestyle.headline: Mentalist Oz Pearlman to headline 2026 White House Correspondents' Dinner
Lifestyle.detail: Mentalist Oz Pearlman will be the entertainer at the 2026 White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Lifestyle.headline: How retirees can generate steady income in today's volatile market
Lifestyle.detail: Experts discuss how retirees can navigate the current volatile market to generate a steady income stream.

Lifestyle.headline: Shoppers shelling out big bucks despite rising chocolate costs
Lifestyle.detail: Despite rising costs, shoppers are still spending money on chocolate.

Lifestyle.headline: United adds TSA wait times to app as DHS shutdown strains airport security lines
Lifestyle.detail: United Airlines is adding TSA wait times to its app to help travelers navigate strained airport security lines due to the DHS shutdown.

Lifestyle.headline: The kindness of strangers: I was taken aback by a rude remark. Then it hit me – she was absolutely right.
Lifestyle.detail: An individual reflects on an interaction with a stranger and realizes the value of the stranger's seemingly rude comment.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The hazard of D.C.'s utility shutoff moratorium
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Janeese Lewis George's idea to address rising energy bills creates a moral hazard.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Veterans spending has surged. Are veterans better off?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial about the surge in veteran spending and whether veterans are better off.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Unexpected surge: On India's industrial growth.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial regarding the unexpected surge in India's industrial growth.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Britain's religious right: using and abusing faith in the pursuit of power.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses the religious right in Britain and their use of faith in the pursuit of power.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial. Barkley's comments reflect how Trump has lost American voters on immigration.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses how Barkley's comments reflect how Trump has lost American voters on immigration.

World.headline: Trump Threatens 'Hell' for Iran Over Hormuz Strait as Deadline Approaches
World.detail: President Trump threatened to strike Iranian power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday. The US president made his threats in an expletive-laden social media post.

World.headline: US Rescues Second Crew Member of Downed F-15E Fighter Jet from Iran
World.detail: The US successfully rescued a second pilot after their fighter jet was shot down in Iran. This was confirmed by President Trump, calling it one of the most daring search and rescue operations in US history.

World.headline: IRGC Navy Says Strait of Hormuz Will 'Never Return to Its Former State' for US, Israel
World.detail: The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy has declared that the Strait of Hormuz will never be the same for the United States and Israel. This statement comes amid heightened tensions and ongoing conflict in the region.

World.headline: Ukraine and Syria to Cooperate on Security, Zelenskyy Says
World.detail: Ukraine and Syria have agreed to cooperate on security matters. President Zelenskyy confirmed the cooperation, though specific details of the agreement remain unclear.

World.headline: Suspected Measles Outbreak Kills Nearly 100 Children in Bangladesh
World.detail: A suspected measles outbreak in Bangladesh has resulted in the deaths of nearly 100 children. Health ministry data indicates a surge in children aged six months to five years exhibiting measles symptoms.

Zimbabwe.headline: Keep promoting peace, love: President
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has commended the Church for its role in national development through participation in various sectors.

Zimbabwe.headline: All set for rollout of new ZiG notes
Zimbabwe.detail: All is in place for the seamless rollout of a new family of ZiG banknotes.

Zimbabwe.headline: Emotional send-off for six crash dead family members
Zimbabwe.detail: A sombre atmosphere engulfed the Zabe area in Ward 3 of Chikomba District yesterday at the sendoff of six members of the Mujuru family who were tragically wiped out in a road accident.

Zimbabwe.headline: Basic necessities now unaffordable… public says, as experts push for US$1.30 fuel prices per litre
Zimbabwe.detail: The public is finding it increasingly difficult to afford basic necessities, leading experts to advocate for fuel prices of US$1.30 per litre.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mujuru family members laid to rest
Zimbabwe.detail: The Mujuru family members who passed away were laid to rest.
